description: MatSlop MATLAB Feature-Parity Roadmap — 7 phases from layout fixes through cross-platform packaging, bringing MatSlop to production-ready MATLAB alternative.

userStories.title: Restructure main layout so Command Window is below the Editor only
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Command Window to sit directly beneath the editor so it doesn't compete with the Workspace or File Browser for horizontal space.
acceptanceCriteria: Default layout: File Browser (left), Editor (center-top) with Command Window (center-bottom), Workspace (right)
Command Window does NOT extend under the File Browser or Workspace columns
Resizing the editor vertically shrinks/grows the Command Window
Layout persistence still works after this restructure
Existing layout regression tests pass
Playwright E2E test verifies Command Window bounding box is horizontally contained within the editor column
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Restructured App.tsx layout into 3 outer columns (FileBrowser | CenterColumn | RightColumn). Center column has Editor on top and Command Window beneath. Added data-testid='editor-column' and data-testid='editor-panel'. Added tests/e2e/layout.spec.ts with bounding box assertions.

userStories.title: Fix live-script cell drag-and-drop reordering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag cells by their grip handle to reorder them in a live script.
acceptanceCriteria: Grabbing the grip handle and dragging a cell moves it above/below other cells
Drop zones highlight during drag
Cell order change persists to the .mls file on save
Playwright test: drag cell 2 above cell 1 and assert DOM order changed
Outputs follow their cells (no orphaned results after reorder)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extracted pure reorderCells() helper in editorTypes.ts. LiveScriptEditor now uses it. Added data-testid='ls-cell'/'ls-drop-zone'/'ls-cell-drag-handle' plus data-cell-id attributes. CSS enlarges drop zones while dragging. Added unit tests (reorderCells) and Playwright test that simulates HTML5 drag events to drag cell 2 above cell 1 and asserts DOM order changed.

userStories.title: Apply MATLAB-default layout preset on first launch
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the initial layout to match MATLAB's defaults so I feel at home.
acceptanceCriteria: First-run layout: File Browser left, Editor + Command Window center, Workspace right
'Reset Layout' menu item restores this default
Playwright snapshot verifies main-window bounding boxes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: MATLAB-default layout already applied on first launch via defaultLayout in src/main/appConfig.ts (File Browser 220px left, Editor+Command Window center, Workspace 280px right) and the existing 'View → Reset Layout' menu item (resetLayout action in App.tsx restores defaultVisibility+defaultSizes). Added tests/e2e/default-layout.spec.ts with 2 tests: (1) verifies bounding boxes of FileBrowser|EditorColumn|Workspace form the MATLAB-default 3-column arrangement on first launch (launchApp uses an isolated user-data dir so every run is a fresh first launch), (2) hides panels via menu actions then triggers 'resetLayout' and asserts panels return to their original positions.

userStories.title: Refactor live script to CSS grid with global code and output tracks
userStories.description: As a user, I want outputs to flow continuously in a single right-side column across all cells, like MATLAB's Live Editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Live script renders as a 2-column CSS grid: code on left, outputs on right
All cells stack vertically in both columns
Each cell's output pairs align with its code row using grid-row
Outputs no longer nested inside the cell container
Hovering a cell highlights both its code and its output region
Existing fixtures (multiplot.mls, quiver3.mls) still render correctly
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Refactored LiveScriptEditor.tsx to use a 2-track CSS grid (.ls-cells-grid with grid-template-columns: minmax(0,1fr) minmax(0,1fr)). Each code cell now emits two sibling grid items: a .ls-cell-code-side (gutter + editor + delete actions) in column 1 and a .ls-cell-output-col / .ls-cell-output-side in column 2. Outputs are no longer nested inside the code container. Markdown cells, drop zones, and add-cell rows span both columns via grid-column: 1 / -1. Hovering either half toggles a hoveredCellId state that applies .ls-cell-hover to both sides so they highlight together. Added tests/e2e/livescript-grid.spec.ts with 5 Playwright tests covering: (1) grid container has 2 template columns, (2) each code cell has a paired output sibling not nested inside the code container, (3) code and output rects share the same top y (grid-row alignment), (4) hover highlights both halves, (5) multiplot.mls still renders 4 plots in the new layout.

userStories.title: Preserve cell gutter (run button, grip, delete) in grid layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the per-cell controls (run, drag, delete) still attached to each code section in the new grid layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Run button appears in the code column at the cell header
Drag handle remains functional across the grid layout
Delete button accessible per cell
'Add cell' buttons appear between rows, spanning both columns
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The US-004 grid refactor already placed the gutter (drag handle + run button/spinner) and per-cell actions (delete button) inside every .ls-cell-code-side grid item, and add-cell rows already span both columns via `.ls-cells-grid > .ls-add-cell-row { grid-column: 1 / -1 }`. Added tests/e2e/livescript-gutter.spec.ts with 4 Playwright tests that explicitly verify: (1) every code cell has a run control, draggable drag-handle and delete button scoped inside its .ls-cell-code-side container; (2) clicking a cell's delete button removes that cell from the grid; (3) add-cell rows resolve to gridColumnStart=1 and span both grid tracks; (4) the drag handle still reorders cells inside the grid layout (synthetic DragEvents with a shared DataTransfer move the last cell above the first). No production code changes needed — this story is covered by code written during US-004 and is now regression-guarded.

userStories.title: Continuous output column scrolls with code column
userStories.description: As a user, when I scroll the live script, both the code and output columns scroll together.
acceptanceCriteria: Vertical scroll is at the grid-container level, not per-cell
Outputs wider than their code row do not push neighbors out of alignment
Very tall outputs cause the row to expand, not overflow
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Removed per-cell vertical scroll on `.ls-cell-output` (max-height: none; overflow-y: visible; overflow-x: auto) so tall outputs expand their grid row and the existing `.ls-editor` container-level scroll handles the whole live script. Added `overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: visible` on `.ls-cell-output-side` so wide outputs are contained within their grid track (`minmax(0,1fr)`) and cannot widen the track or push neighboring code/output rows out of alignment. Added tests/e2e/livescript-scroll.spec.ts with 3 tests: (1) computed-style assertions that .ls-editor is the scroll container and per-cell output boxes have no max-height or vertical scroll, plus output-side clamps horizontal overflow; (2) inject a 1200px-tall element into the first output and assert the output-side grew to >=1200px, the editor scrollHeight grew correspondingly, no internal vertical scroll was created, and every code/output pair still shares the same top; (3) inject a 5000px-wide element into the first output and assert grid-template-columns is unchanged, grid container width is unchanged, and pair alignment still holds.

userStories.title: Extract plot data from Octave as JSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to convert Octave figure state into a serialized JSON representation so a JavaScript library can re-render it interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement bundled Octave function matslop_export_fig(h) that walks figure handle and emits JSON describing axes, lines, surfaces, meshes, labels, colors, view angles, limits
Supports: plot, plot3, scatter, scatter3, surf, mesh, quiver, quiver3, bar, bar3, contour, contour3, imagesc
JSON schema documented in src/main/plotSchema.ts
Unit tests feed sample figures and verify JSON structure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added src/main/plotSchema.ts with PlotFigure/PlotAxes/PlotSeries TypeScript types covering plot, plot3, scatter, scatter3, surf, mesh, quiver, quiver3, bar, bar3, contour, contour3, imagesc; plus parsePlotFigure(input) that validates JSON strings or objects and downgrades unknown series to {type:'unknown'}. Added bundled Octave function resources/octave-scripts/matslop_export_fig.m that walks a figure handle via get()/findall() and emits a JSON string matching the schema (includes a minimal internal JSON encoder so it works without jsonencode). Added tests/unit/plot-schema.test.ts with 15 passing tests exercising line/line3/scatter/scatter3/surf/mesh/quiver3/bar/bar3/contour/contour3/image, multi-axes (subplot), legend, log scales, error paths, and unknown-type downgrade.

userStories.title: Integrate Plotly.js as JS 3D plotting library
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a JS library that can render the extracted plot data as an interactive 3D canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Short ADR comparing Plotly.js and Three.js written to docs/adr/
Recommendation: Plotly.js (covers 2D + 3D, rotate/zoom/pan built-in)
Plotly.js dependency added, bundle size impact documented
PlotRenderer React component accepts JSON schema and renders a Plotly figure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ADR at docs/adr/0001-plotly-vs-threejs.md — recommends Plotly.js because it covers every roadmap trace type and ships rotate/zoom/pan/hover/modebar/downloadImage built-in. Bundle size documented: plotly.js-dist-min@3.5.0 is 4.7 MB minified. Added plotly.js-dist-min and @types/plotly.js deps. Added minimal `declare module 'plotly.js-dist-min'` to src/renderer/env.d.ts. Added src/renderer/editor/plotlyAdapter.ts with a pure `figureToPlotly(figure): {data, layout, config}` (plus rgbToCss) that maps every roadmap series type — line/line3/scatter/scatter3/surface/mesh/quiver(→2 scatter traces)/quiver3(→cone)/bar/bar3(→scatter3d verticals)/contour/contour3/image(→heatmap) — and emits Plotly subplot xaxis/xaxis2/scene/scene2 for multi-axes. Added src/renderer/editor/PlotRenderer.tsx React wrapper that lazy-imports plotly.js-dist-min via dynamic import (keeps the 4.7 MB bundle out of the initial renderer chunk), mounts a div with data-testid='plot-renderer', Plotly.react on mount/update, Plotly.purge on unmount. Added tests/unit/plotly-adapter.test.ts (20 passing tests). `npm run build` succeeds (renderer bundle unchanged at ~789 KB because Plotly is lazy-loaded).

userStories.title: Replace static PNG plots with interactive renderer in live scripts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to rotate, zoom, and pan plots with my mouse directly in the live script output.
acceptanceCriteria: Live script cells use PlotRenderer instead of PNG InteractivePlot
3D plots are rotatable by mouse drag
2D and 3D plots are zoomable with scroll wheel
Panning with Shift+drag (or middle-click drag)
Home button resets view
Playwright test: open quiver3.mls, verify a Plotly canvas renders with interactive controls
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Wired the end-to-end interactive plot pipeline. LiveScriptEditor's runCellStatements now also emits `matslop_export_fig(gcf())` for each plot snapshot, writes the JSON to a sibling temp file via fopen/fwrite, and prints a `__MSLP_FIG_JSON_FILE__:<line>:<path>` marker. A new preload API `figuresReadTextFile` (ipcMain handler `figures:readTextFile`) reads the UTF-8 JSON back into the renderer and attaches it as `plotJson` on `LiveScriptCellFigure`. `InlinePlots` now parses `plotJson` with `parsePlotFigure` and renders via the Plotly-backed `PlotRenderer` (data-testid `plot-renderer` + wrapper `ls-inline-plot`), falling back to the PNG `InteractivePlot` on parse failure. OctaveProcessManager takes an optional `scriptsDir` arg and addpath()s it during its init script so `matslop_export_fig` is discoverable; main/index.ts passes `getMatslopScriptsDir()` (new helper in octaveConfig.ts that resolves `resources/octave-scripts` in both dev and packaged builds). package.json `extraResources` now ships `resources/octave-scripts/**/*.m`. Rotate/zoom/pan/home are Plotly-native modebar controls — no custom code needed. New tests: `tests/unit/livescript-interactive-plot.test.ts` (3 cases: quiver3 JSON → cone trace, 2D line → scatter, malformed JSON rejection drives PNG fallback) + `tests/e2e/livescript-interactive-plots.spec.ts` (opens quiver3.mls, asserts `plot-renderer` wrappers mount, the Plotly `.js-plotly-plot` is visible, and the modebar Reset/Home button is present). Files changed: src/renderer/editor/editorTypes.ts, src/renderer/editor/LiveScriptEditor.tsx, src/main/octaveProcess.ts, src/main/octaveConfig.ts, src/main/index.ts, src/preload/index.ts, src/renderer/env.d.ts, package.json, tests/unit/livescript-interactive-plot.test.ts (new), tests/e2e/livescript-interactive-plots.spec.ts (new), scripts/ralph/prd.json, scripts/ralph/progress.txt.

userStories.title: Data cursor / tooltip on plot points
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a point on a plot and see its (x, y, z) value, like MATLAB's data cursor mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering a data point shows a tooltip with coordinates
Clicking pins the tooltip until clicked away
Works for 2D and 3D plots
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Export current plot view as PNG/SVG
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save the interactive plot as it currently looks (including my rotation) to a file.
acceptanceCriteria: Save button exports current Plotly canvas as PNG and SVG
Default filename derived from figure title
Dialog lets user pick format

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Detached plot window
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pop a plot out into its own OS window for larger-screen interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Button on plot toolbar opens plot in a new BrowserWindow
Detached window is fully interactive (same controls)
Closing the detached window returns focus to main window
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added main-process registry (detachedFigures map + plot:openDetached / plot:getDetachedFigure IPCs) that spawns a new BrowserWindow loading ?detachedFigureId=<id>. main.tsx detects the query param and mounts a new DetachedPlot component instead of <App/>. DetachedPlot fetches the figure via IPC, runs parsePlotFigure, and renders a full-viewport PlotRenderer with canDetach={false}. Added Detach button (data-testid='plot-detach-btn') on PlotRenderer that serializes the current figure via window.matslop.plotOpenDetached. Closing the detached window frees the map entry and refocuses main. Added tests/e2e/detached-plot.spec.ts (2 tests, both passing) using a minimal synthetic figure so no Octave is needed.

userStories.title: Handle plot export failures gracefully
userStories.description: As a user, if Octave can't serialize a figure (unsupported type), I want to see a clear message instead of a broken UI.
acceptanceCriteria: If matslop_export_fig fails, fall back to PNG rendering with a warning banner
Warning explains which plot type is unsupported and links to a help doc
No crash, no empty canvas
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented plot export failure handling via Octave __MSLP_FIG_ERR__ marker + buildPlotWarning helper + warning banner in InlinePlots; falls back to PNG on fatal errors, shows advisory for unknown series types.
